patterns.definition: Agent spends an edit/tool-call authoring a generic custom helper script (e.g. edit_file.py / view_file.py) before reading or editing the actual task file, independent of any demonstrated need for it over built-in commands.
positive_indicators: an EDIT event whose payload writes a *.py helper that re-implements view/replace
occurs before first READ of the target source file
immediately follows the harness 'create tools' reflection nudge
required_evidence: ≥1 EDIT/WRITE event creating a helper script not part of the repo
no preceding evidence that built-in read/edit was insufficient
exclusions: editing the actual repo file under test
writing a reproduction script for the issue (that is verification, see VERIFICATION workloads)
patterns.distinguishing_rule: PREEMPTIVE_HELPER_TOOL_BUILD = creating the helper. SELF_BUILT_TOOL_BREAKAGE = that helper later erroring and needing rework. Reproduction scripts are excluded because they exercise the bug, not just edit files.
patterns.notes: Strongly correlated with one solver interface; the harness 'Reflect... create tools' message after every step appears to induce it. Cheap individually but a fixed per-run tax, and it is the precursor to SELF_BUILT_TOOL_BREAKAGE.

patterns.definition: The agent's own helper tool fails at use (encoding error, wrong usage signature) and must be re-created or worked around, consuming cycles that would not exist had a built-in been used.
positive_indicators: TOOL_ERROR raised from the agent-authored script
followed by re-writing/patching that same script
then retrying the original intended view/edit
required_evidence: a TOOL_ERROR whose traceback points at the self-authored helper
a subsequent EDIT re-creating/fixing that helper
exclusions: TOOL_ERROR from repo code or harness commands (those are environment, not self-built)
first failed attempt at a legitimate command typo
patterns.distinguishing_rule: Breakage of the AGENT'S OWN script = this pattern; failure of harness/built-in tooling (e.g. _split_string SyntaxError, str_replace_editor crash) = ENVIRONMENT_TOOL_FAILURE (uncovered).
patterns.notes: django-13513 is the clean exemplar: create [1] -> UnicodeDecodeError [2] -> rewrite with utf-8 [3] -> retry [4].

patterns.definition: Two or more consecutive file-location searches return no matches for the same conceptual target because the search tool's semantics are misapplied (find_file matches filenames, not symbols/content), before the agent pivots to a working strategy.
positive_indicators: ≥2 SEARCH events with 'No matches found' for the same target
same tool form repeated with only the query string tweaked
eventual pivot to find/grep/ls that succeeds
required_evidence: ≥2 SEARCH events returning empty for semantically the same goal
no new evidence acquired between them
exclusions: a single failed search then immediate pivot (normal)
a new search after FALSIFYING a hypothesis (justified)
searches across genuinely different targets
patterns.distinguishing_rule: THRASH = locating WHICH FILE via repeated no-match filename searches. BLIND_INFILE_NAVIGATION = file already open, wrong line jumped to. REDUNDANT_FILE_REREAD = re-viewing content already seen.
patterns.notes: Tightly tied to the open/goto/find_file interface. The agent repeatedly treats find_file as a symbol/content search; 2-3 wasted no-match rounds per affected run before falling back to `find . -name` or `grep -r`.

patterns.definition: After opening a file, the agent jumps to a guessed line number (goto N) that does not contain the target, then must run an in-file search and re-navigate, wasting the first jump.
positive_indicators: open -> goto <guessed line> showing irrelevant code -> search_file -> goto <correct line>
required_evidence: a goto whose observed window does not contain the target symbol
a following in-file search that then yields the real line
exclusions: goto directly to a line already located by a prior search (efficient)
scrolling within a region already identified as relevant
patterns.distinguishing_rule: Occurs AFTER the correct file is open and is about line-within-file; THRASH is about which file; REDUNDANT_FILE_REREAD re-shows identical content rather than guessing a new wrong line.
patterns.notes: django-16100: open options.py [2] -> goto 1500 [3] (function not there) -> search_file finds 1914 [4] -> goto 1914 [5]. Lower-frequency than THRASH in the visible windows; many instances likely past truncation.

patterns.definition: A large number of edit attempts concentrated on a small set of files (high patch_attempts, low files_modified) with no intervening test execution that would supply new evidence to justify each successive edit.
positive_indicators: patch_attempts >> files_modified
targeted_test_count == 0 / first_test_step == null while editing continues
repeated EDIT events on the same path with no TEST between
required_evidence: no TEST event separating consecutive edits to the same file
exclusions: edit -> failing test -> fix (normal iteration)
edits to genuinely different files (cross-file work)
high files_modified that is actually inflated by helper/repro scripts (see false positives)
patterns.distinguishing_rule: CHURN = repeated edits to the SAME region with no new evidence between. VERIFICATION_GAP = absence of ANY test across the whole run (may have few edits). STAGNATION = exact-duplicate calls / no-new-evidence streak not necessarily edits.
patterns.notes: django-16560: patch_attempts=22 on files_modified=1 with first_test_step=null. sympy-15599: patch_attempts=39, terminated LimitsExceeded. Mostly inferred from features since the churn lies past the visible window — flagged as outcome-collapse risk.

patterns.definition: The run modifies source and terminates 'Submitted' without ever executing a test OR a reproduction script that exercises the changed code.
positive_indicators: files_modified>0 and patch_attempts>0
first_test_step == null
no EXECUTE event running a repro that imports the changed path
termination_reason == Submitted
required_evidence: zero TEST events and zero repro-execution events across the trace
exclusions: runs that DID execute a reproduction script (e.g. astropy-7606 [3], astropy-8872 [3]) — those verified via repro
runs killed by environment before they could test
patterns.distinguishing_rule: GAP is defined by TOTAL absence of any execution-based check before submit; CHURN is about edit repetition regardless of submit. Excludes repro-script verification, which is execution evidence even if targeted_test_count==0.
patterns.notes: OUTCOME-COLLAPSE RISK: 'no test' is not 'failed'. Kept because it is a resource/process pattern observable without knowing the outcome — the agent committed edits it never exercised. Truncation means a late test could exist; relying on first_test_step==null feature.

patterns.definition: High rate of exact-duplicate tool calls and/or long streaks of actions producing no new evidence, indicating the agent re-issuing equivalent actions without advancing state.
positive_indicators: exact_duplicate_call_rate elevated
longest_no_new_evidence_streak large
repeated identical commands in visible window
required_evidence: duplicates not separated by a state change (edit/test/install)
exclusions: re-running a command after the environment changed (install, edit) — justified
a couple of orientation re-lists
patterns.distinguishing_rule: STAGNATION is about exact-duplicate / no-progress calls of ANY kind; CHURN is specifically repeated edits; REDUNDANT_FILE_REREAD is specifically repeated reads of same content.
patterns.notes: django-7530 is extreme: exact_duplicate_call_rate=2.38 and longest_no_new_evidence_streak=61, terminating submitted(exit_cost). exit_cost/LimitsExceeded co-occurs -> partial outcome-collapse risk, flagged.

patterns.definition: Re-reading the same file or region already viewed, with no intervening edit/test that would have changed it.
positive_indicators: duplicate_file_reads elevated
same path opened/cat'd multiple times
no edit to that file between reads
exclusions: re-read after editing that file (to confirm the edit)
reading different regions of a large file
patterns.distinguishing_rule: REREAD = identical content re-fetched; NAVIGATION = guessing a wrong new line; STAGNATION = duplicate calls of any tool type. Re-read strictly after an edit to the same file is excluded (verification).
patterns.notes: Largely inferred from duplicate_file_reads (scikit-9288=12, xarray-6461=9, django-15732=5) since the repeats occur past truncation; visible-window evidence is partial.

patterns.definition: A large fraction of the run is consumed by exploration/reading/environment activity before the first edit, AND the run fails to converge (terminates on cost/limit or a long no-progress streak) rather than landing a focused fix.
positive_indicators: first_edit_step late relative to n_events
high environment_setup_events / search_call_count
termination exit_cost / LimitsExceeded OR long no-new-evidence streak
required_evidence: first_edit_step is a large fraction of total events
exclusions: genuinely localization-gated tasks that still converged to a focused patch (e.g. matplotlib-25775 broad search but submitted) — that is workload, not waste
late edit that immediately succeeds
patterns.distinguishing_rule: Requires BOTH late-first-edit AND a non-convergence signal; pure late-first-edit on a hard-to-localize task that still lands a clean patch is the LOCALIZATION_GATED workload, not waste.
patterns.notes: psf-requests-6028: first_edit_step=47 of 76 events, environment_setup_events=59, exit_cost. Guarded against outcome-collapse by requiring the non-convergence co-signal, not failure per se.

uncovered_patterns.name: ENVIRONMENT_TOOL_FAILURE
uncovered_patterns.definition: Harness/built-in tooling (not agent-authored) breaks: the _split_string 'future feature annotations' SyntaxError on edit, str_replace_editor crashing, command timeouts, missing deps (tox/xdist).
uncovered_patterns.note: Per rule 6 these are environment failures, NOT agent reasoning/edit waste; recovery cost is real but should not be attributed to the model's choices.

uncovered_patterns.name: REPRO_VERSION_DRIFT_DEADEND
uncovered_patterns.definition: Agent writes a reproduction script that does not reproduce because the checked-out dev version already differs from the reported version; time spent before realizing.
uncovered_patterns.note: Could be waste or necessary diligence; cannot decide without later events.

uncovered_patterns.name: MID_RUN_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL
uncovered_patterns.definition: Agent installs a missing package to proceed (pip install).

uncovered_patterns.name: POST_EDIT_CONFIRM_READ
uncovered_patterns.definition: Agent re-reads the just-edited region to confirm the change landed.

needs_unavailable_info.why: Nearly every transcript is cut at ~event 4-7 while n_events is 11-108. The bulk of patch_attempts, repeated tests, churn and recovery occur AFTER the visible window. Patterns 5-9 lean on deterministic features precisely because the events are invisible.
Cannot size the actual resource cost of waste (a cheap duplicate ls vs an expensive re-read of a 2000-line file).
Cannot confirm whether a repeated search was a justified hypothesis-falsification or aimless thrash without the agent's intent.
Cannot validate that VERIFICATION_GAP / PATCH_CHURN actually correlate with failure vs. correct one-shot fixes.
No timing; cannot separate slow-but-progressing from stalled.

outcome_collapse_risks.risk: Easily becomes a proxy for 'the run that failed'. Mitigation: defined purely as 'edited code never executed before submit' — observable without the outcome — and excludes runs that ran a repro script.
'Never converged' can collapse into 'failed'. Mitigation: require repeated edits to the SAME file with no test between, not just many edits.

exit_cost/LimitsExceeded co-signal (django-7530, sympy-15599) is close to an outcome. Mitigation: primary evidence is exact_duplicate_call_rate / no-new-evidence streak, which are process metrics independent of pass/fail.

Late first edit may just mean a hard-to-localize task. Mitigation: requires a co-occurring non-convergence signal AND is explicitly distinguished from the LOCALIZATION_GATED workload (matplotlib-25775 excluded).

false_positive_examples.why_not_waste: For a one-line ensure_ascii change, 13 'modified files' almost certainly counts the created edit_file.py helper, a reproduce script, and possibly .pyc/test scaffolding — not 13 source files churned. files_modified must not be read as multi-file churn.
Implausible as real source edits for a single core/mod.py fix; inflated by helper/repro/test artifacts and re-writes of the same file. Needs path-level inspection before calling churn.
The task is an enhancement spanning text.py and multiple backend modules; broad search is warranted breadth, not redundant search.
Tests were re-run after installing pytest-xdist [4] — the environment changed between runs, so repetition is justified, not redundant.
The empty grep at [3] FALSIFIED 'is _base_manager already used here?'; the next broader grep [4] is evidence-driven, not thrash.

justified_repeat_examples.why: Reads itrs.py, intermediate_rotation_transforms.py, utils.py, altaz.py — distinct files all required because the task adds cross-frame transforms; not redundant re-reads.
Ran tests, hit missing xdist, installed it, re-ran — each test repetition followed a state change.
Second/broader grep issued only after the first returned empty, falsifying the local-usage hypothesis.
Re-ran the reproduction with larger sample/different probe after the first repro showed no signal — new evidence sought, not blind repetition.
